columns.Name: COMMUNICATION_ID
columns.Comments: Unique Id for identifying the communication message

columns.Name: TRANSACTION_ID
columns.Comments: Id of transaction for which the communication is for

columns.Name: COMMUNICATION_DATA
columns.Comments: Communication data in Json format

columns.Name: USER_GUID
columns.Comments: Guid of the user to whom the communication is intented.

columns.Name: MODULEIDENTIFIER
columns.Comments: Module Identifier of the process related to what communication is sent

columns.Name: PROCESS_ID
columns.Comments: Process Id of the process related to what the communication is intented to.

columns.Name: SUBJECT_CODE
columns.Comments: Subject code related to message being sent.

columns.Name: OBJECT_CODE
columns.Comments: Object code related to the message being sent.

columns.Name: SUBJECT
columns.Comments: Name of the component which triggered the notification

columns.Name: SUBJECT_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of the component which triggered the notification.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_INSTANCE_ID
columns.Comments: Correlation id sent by the triggering component to identify the message record.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_EXPIRATION_TIME
columns.Comments: Message Expiration time which will determine the life time of the message.

columns.Name: OBJECT
columns.Comments: Name of the object which is getting modified and related to what communication is sent

columns.Name: OBJECT_ID
columns.Comments: Id of the primary key of the object row related to which the communication is sent out

columns.Name: STATUS
columns.Comments: Status of the communication. It will have values like pending completed failed etc

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of the enterprise for filtering the records.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: IS_MSG_GETTING_PURGED
columns.Comments: Flag to indicate if communication message is a candidate for getting purged.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_HEADER
columns.Comments: Indicates the header of the message to be shown in the auditing ui.
